 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 15→ ****************************************************************************************/
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.Intent
 21→import android.net.Uri
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Message
 24→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 25→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 26→import androidx.core.app.TaskStackBuilder
 27→import androidx.core.content.FileProvider
 28→import androidx.core.content.IntentCompat
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandler.Companion.storeMessage
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandlerMessage
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.ScopedStorageService
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.reviewer.ReviewerFragment
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.worker.SyncWorker
 36→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 37→import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 38→import com.ichi2.utils.FileUtil
 39→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.handleFileImport
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.isInvalidViewIntent
 41→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.showImportUnsuccessfulDialog
 42→import com.ichi2.utils.IntentUtil.resolveMimeType
 43→import com.ichi2.utils.NetworkUtils
 44→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions
 45→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions.hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission
 46→import com.ichi2.utils.copyToClipboard
 47→import com.ichi2.utils.trimToLength
 48→import timber.log.Timber
 49→import java.io.File
 50→import kotlin.math.max
 51→import kotlin.math.min
 52→
 53→/**
 54→ * Class which handles how the application responds to different intents, forcing it to always be single task,
 55→ * but allowing custom behavior depending on the intent
 56→ * It inherits from [AbstractIntentHandler]
 57→ *
 58→ * @author Tim
 59→ */
 60→class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
 61→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 62→        // Note: This is our entry point from the launcher with intent: android.intent.action.MAIN
 63→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 64→        val intent = intent
 65→        Timber.v(intent.toString())
 66→        val reloadIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
 67→        reloadIntent.setDataAndType(getIntent().data, getIntent().type)
 68→        val action = intent.action
 69→        // #6157 - We want to block actions that need permissions we don't have, but not the default case
 70→        // as this requires nothing
 71→        val runIfStoragePermissions = { runnable: () -> Unit -> performActionIfStorageAccessible(reloadIntent, action) { runnable() } }
 72→        val launchType = getLaunchType(intent)
 73→        // TODO block the UI with some kind of ProgressDialog instead of cancelling the sync work
 74→        if (requiresCollectionAccess(launchType)) {
 75→            SyncWorker.cancel(this)
 76→        }
 77→        when (launchType) {
 78→            LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT ->
 79→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 80→                    handleFileImport(fileIntent, reloadIntent, action)
 81→                    finish()
 82→                }
 83→            LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT ->
 84→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 85→                    onSelectedCsvForImport(fileIntent)
 86→                    finish()
 87→                }
 88→            LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT ->
 89→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 90→                    handleImageImport(intent)
 91→                    finish()
 92→                }
 93→            LaunchType.SYNC -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleSyncIntent(reloadIntent, action) }
 94→            LaunchType.REVIEW -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleReviewIntent(reloadIntent, intent) }
 95→            LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW -> {
 96→                Timber.d("onCreate() performing default action")
 97→                launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
 98→            }
 99→            LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> {
100→                copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent)
101→                finish()
102→            }
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent: Intent) {
107→        Timber.i("Copying debug info to clipboard")
108→        // null string is handled by copyToClipboard in try-catch
109→        this.copyToClipboard(
110→            text = (intent.getStringExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA)!!),
111→            failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info,
112→        )
113→    }
114→
115→    private val fileIntent: Intent
116→        get() {
117→            return if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
118→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Intent::class.java) ?: intent
119→            } else {
120→                intent
121→            }
122→        }
123→
124→    /**
125→     * Execute the runnable if one of the two following conditions are satisfied:
126→     *
127→     *  * AnkiDroid is using an app-private directory to store user data
128→     *  * AnkiDroid is using a legacy directory to store user data but has access to it since storage permission
129→     * has been granted (as long as AnkiDroid targeted API < 30, requested legacy storage, and has not been uninstalled since)
130→     *
131→     */
132→    @NeedsTest("clicking a file in 'Files' to import")
133→    private fun performActionIfStorageAccessible(
134→        reloadIntent: Intent,
135→        action: String?,
136→        block: () -> Unit,
137→    ) {
138→        if (grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
139→            Timber.i("User has storage permissions. Running intent: %s", action)
140→            block()
141→        } else {
142→            Timber.i("No Storage Permission, cancelling intent '%s'", action)
143→            launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun handleReviewIntent(
148→        reloadIntent: Intent,
149→        reviewerIntent: Intent,
150→    ) {
151→        val deckId = intent.getLongExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, 0)
152→        Timber.i("Handling intent to review deck '%d'", deckId)
153→
154→        val reviewIntent =
155→            if (sharedPrefs().getBoolean("newReviewer", false)) {
156→                ReviewerFragment.getIntent(this)
157→            } else {
158→                Intent(this, Reviewer::class.java).apply {
159→                    addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
160→                }
161→            }
162→        CollectionManager.getColUnsafe().decks.select(deckId)
163→        // Clean the stack out under the reviewer to avoid any incorrect activities / dialogs /
164→        // data state from prior app usage showing after reviewer exits if going to reviewer directly
165→        TaskStackBuilder
166→            .create(applicationContext)
167→            .addNextIntent(reloadIntent)
168→            .addNextIntent(reviewIntent)
169→            .startActivities()
170→        finish()
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun handleSyncIntent(
174→        reloadIntent: Intent,
175→        action: String?,
176→    ) {
177→        Timber.i("Handling Sync Intent")
178→        sendDoSyncMsg()
179→        reloadIntent.action = action
180→        reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
181→        startActivity(reloadIntent)
182→        finish()
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun handleFileImport(
186→        intent: Intent,
187→        reloadIntent: Intent,
188→        action: String?,
189→    ) {
190→        Timber.i("Handling file import")
191→        if (!hasShownAppIntro()) {
192→            Timber.i("Trying to import a file when the app was not started at all")
193→            showThemedToast(this, R.string.app_not_initialized_new, false)
194→            return
195→        }
196→        val importResult = handleFileImport(this, intent)
197→        // attempt to delete the downloaded deck if it is a shared deck download import
198→        if (intent.hasExtra(SharedDecksDownloadFragment.EXTRA_IS_SHARED_DOWNLOAD)) {
199→            deleteDownloadedDeck(intent.data)
200→        }
201→
202→        // Start DeckPicker if we correctly processed ACTION_VIEW
203→        if (importResult.isSuccess) {
204→            deleteImportedDeck(intent.data?.path)
205→            reloadIntent.action = action
206→            reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
207→            startActivity(reloadIntent)
208→            finish()
209→        } else {
210→            Timber.i("File import failed")
211→            // Don't import the file if it didn't load properly or doesn't have apkg extension
212→            showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, true)
213→        }
214→    }
215→
216→    private fun deleteImportedDeck(path: String?) {
217→        try {
218→            val file = File(path!!)
219→            val fileUri =
220→                applicationContext?.let {
221→                    FileProvider.getUriForFile(
222→                        it,
223→                        it.applicationContext?.packageName + ".apkgfileprovider",
224→                        File(it.getExternalFilesDir(FileUtil.getDownloadDirectory()), file.name),
225→                    )
226→                }
227→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
228→            contentResolver.delete(fileUri!!, null, null)
229→            Timber.i("onCreate() import successful and downloaded file deleted")
230→        } catch (e: Exception) {
231→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate() import successful and cannot delete file")
232→        }
233→    }
234→
235→    private fun handleImageImport(data: Intent) {
236→        val imageUri =
237→            if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
238→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri::class.java)
239→            } else {
240→                data.data
241→            }
242→
243→        val imageOcclusionIntentBuilder = ImageOcclusionIntentBuilder(this)
244→        val intentImageOcclusion = imageOcclusionIntentBuilder.buildIntent(imageUri)
245→
246→        TaskStackBuilder
247→            .create(this)
248→            .addNextIntentWithParentStack(Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java))
249→            .addNextIntent(intentImageOcclusion)
250→            .startActivities()
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun deleteDownloadedDeck(sharedDeckUri: Uri?) {
254→        if (sharedDeckUri == null) {
255→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded a shared deck but uri was null when trying to delete its file")
256→            return
257→        }
258→
259→        try {
260→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
261→            contentResolver.delete(sharedDeckUri, null, null)
262→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded shared deck deleted")
263→        } catch (e: Exception) {
264→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate: failed to delete downloaded shared deck")
265→        }
266→    }
267→
268→    private fun launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent: Intent) {
269→        // Launcher intents should start DeckPicker if no other task exists,
270→        // otherwise go to previous task
271→        Timber.i("Launching DeckPicker")
272→        reloadIntent.action = Intent.ACTION_MAIN
273→        reloadIntent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_LAUNCHER)
274→        reloadIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
275→        startActivityIfNeeded(reloadIntent, 0)
276→        finish()
277→    }
278→
279→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: Also extract the parameters into here to reduce coupling
280→    @VisibleForTesting
281→    enum class LaunchType {
282→        DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
283→
284→        /** colpkg/apkg/unknown */
285→        FILE_IMPORT,
286→
287→        /** csv/tsv */
288→        TEXT_IMPORT,
289→
290→        /** image */
291→        IMAGE_IMPORT,
292→
293→        SYNC,
294→        REVIEW,
295→        COPY_DEBUG_INFO,
296→    }
297→
298→    companion object {
299→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT = "com.ichi2.anki.COPY_DEBUG_INFO"
300→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA = "clip_data"
301→
302→        private fun isValidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean {
303→            // Negating a negative because we want to call specific attention to the fact that it's invalid
304→            // #6312 - Smart Launcher provided an empty ACTION_VIEW, no point in importing here.
305→            return !isInvalidViewIntent(intent)
306→        }
307→
308→        /** Checks whether storage permissions are granted on the device. If the device is not using legacy storage,
309→         *  it verifies if the app has been granted the necessary storage access permission.
310→         *  @return `true`: if granted, otherwise `false` and shows a missing permission toast
311→         */
312→        fun grantedStoragePermissions(
313→            context: Context,
314→            showToast: Boolean,
315→        ): Boolean {
316→            val granted =
317→                !ScopedStorageService.isLegacyStorage(context) ||
318→                    hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission(context) ||
319→                    Permissions.isExternalStorageManagerCompat()
320→
321→            if (!granted && showToast) {
322→                showThemedToast(context, context.getString(R.string.intent_handler_failed_no_storage_permission), false)
323→            }
324→
325→            return granted
326→        }
327→
328→        @VisibleForTesting
329→        @CheckResult
330→        fun getLaunchType(intent: Intent): LaunchType {
331→            val action = intent.action
332→            return if (action == Intent.ACTION_SEND || Intent.ACTION_VIEW == action && isValidViewIntent(intent)) {
333→                val mimeType = intent.resolveMimeType()
334→                when {
335→                    mimeType?.startsWith("image/") == true -> LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT
336→                    mimeType == "text/plain" ||
337→                        mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
338→                        mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" ||
339→                        mimeType == "text/csv" ||
340→                        mimeType == "application/csv" ||
341→                        mimeType == "application/x-csv" ||
342→                        mimeType == "text/x-csv" ||
343→                        mimeType == "application/vnd.ms-excel" ||
344→                        mimeType == "application/vnd.msexcel" ||
345→                        mimeType == "text/anytext" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
346→                    else -> LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT
347→                }
348→            } else if ("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" == action) {
349→                LaunchType.SYNC
350→            } else if (intent.hasExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID)) {
351→                LaunchType.REVIEW
352→            } else if (action == CLIPBOARD_INTENT) {
353→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO
354→            } else {
355→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW
356→            }
357→        }
358→
359→        /**
360→         * Send a Message to AnkiDroidApp so that the DialogMessageHandler forces a sync
361→         */
362→        fun sendDoSyncMsg() {
363→            // Store the message in AnkiDroidApp message holder, which is loaded later in AnkiActivity.onResume
364→            storeMessage(DoSync().toMessage())
365→        }
366→
367→        fun copyStringToClipboardIntent(
368→            context: Context,
369→            textToCopy: String,
370→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).also {
371→            it.action = CLIPBOARD_INTENT
372→            // max length for an intent is 500KB.
373→            // 25000 * 2 (bytes per char) = 50,000 bytes <<< 500KB
374→            it.putExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA, textToCopy.trimToLength(25000))
375→        }
376→
377→        fun requiresCollectionAccess(launchType: LaunchType): Boolean =
378→            when (launchType) {
379→                LaunchType.SYNC,
380→                LaunchType.REVIEW,
381→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
382→                LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT,
383→                LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT,
384→                LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT,
385→                -> true
386→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> false
387→            }
388→
389→        class DoSync :
390→            DialogHandlerMessage(
391→                which = WhichDialogHandler.MSG_DO_SYNC,
392→                analyticName = "DoSyncDialog",
393→            ) {
394→            override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
395→                // we may be called via any AnkiActivity but sync is a DeckPicker thing
396→                if (activity !is DeckPicker) {
397→                    showError(
398→                        activity,
399→                        activity.getString(R.string.something_wrong),
400→                        ClassCastException(activity.javaClass.simpleName + " is not " + DeckPicker.javaClass.simpleName),
401→                        true,
402→                    )
403→                    return
404→                }
405→                // let's be clear about the type now that we've checked
406→                val deckPicker = activity
407→
408→                val preferences = deckPicker.sharedPrefs()
409→                val res = deckPicker.resources
410→                val hkey = preferences.getString("hkey", "")
411→                val millisecondsSinceLastSync = millisecondsSinceLastSync(preferences)
412→                val limited = millisecondsSinceLastSync < INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL
413→                if (!limited && hkey!!.isNotEmpty() && NetworkUtils.isOnline) {
414→                    deckPicker.sync()
415→                } else {
416→                    val err = res.getString(R.string.sync_error)
417→                    if (limited) {
418→                        val remainingTimeInSeconds =
419→                            max((INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL - millisecondsSinceLastSync) / 1000, 1)
420→                        // getQuantityString needs an int
421→                        val remaining = min(Int.MAX_VALUE.toLong(), remainingTimeInSeconds).toInt()
422→                        val message =
423→                            res.getQuantityString(
424→                                R.plurals.sync_automatic_sync_needs_more_time,
425→                                remaining,
426→                                remaining,
427→                            )
428→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(err, message, Channel.SYNC)
429→                    } else {
430→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(
431→                            err,
432→                            res.getString(R.string.youre_offline),
433→                            Channel.SYNC,
434→                        )
435→                    }
436→                }
437→                deckPicker.finish()
438→            }
439→
440→            override fun toMessage(): Message = emptyMessage(this.what)
441→
442→            companion object {
443→                const val INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL =
444→                    (
445→                        2 * 60000 // 2min minimum sync interval
446→                    ).toLong()
447→            }
448→        }
449→
450→        /**
451→         * Returns an intent to review a specific deck.
452→         * This does not states which reviewer to use, instead IntentHandler will choose whether to use the
453→         * legacy or the new reviewer based on the "newReviewer" preference.
454→         * It is expected to be used from widget, shortcut, reminders but not from ankidroid directly because of the CLEAR_TOP flag.
455→         */
456→        fun intentToReviewDeckFromShorcuts(
457→            context: Context,
458→            deckId: DeckId,
459→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).apply {
460→            setAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
461→            putExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, deckId)
462→        }
463→    }
464→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.webkit.WebView
 23→import androidx.activity.OnBackPressedCallback
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.SingleFragmentActivity
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.hideShowButtonCss
 28→import com.ichi2.themes.setTransparentStatusBar
 29→
 30→/**
 31→ * Anki page used to import text/csv files
 32→ */
 33→class CsvImporter : PageFragment() {
 34→    override fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): PageWebViewClient {
 35→        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
 36→        val backCallback =
 37→            object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
 38→                override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
 39→                    CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
 40→                    // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
 41→                    remove()
 42→                }
 43→            }
 44→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 45→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
 46→        return CsvImporterWebViewClient(backCallback)
 47→    }
 48→
 49→    override fun onViewCreated(
 50→        view: View,
 51→        savedInstanceState: Bundle?,
 52→    ) {
 53→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 54→        requireActivity().setTransparentStatusBar()
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    inner class CsvImporterWebViewClient(
 58→        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback,
 59→    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
 60→        /**
 61→         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
 62→         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
 63→         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
 64→         */
 65→        private var isDone = false
 66→
 67→        override fun onPageFinished(
 68→            view: WebView?,
 69→            url: String?,
 70→        ) {
 71→            view!!.evaluateJavascript(hideShowButtonCss) {
 72→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
 73→            }
 74→        }
 75→
 76→        override fun onLoadResource(
 77→            view: WebView?,
 78→            url: String?,
 79→        ) {
 80→            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
 81→            backCallback.isEnabled =
 82→                when {
 83→                    url == null -> false
 84→                    url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
 85→                    url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
 86→                        isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
 87→                        false
 88→                    }
 89→                    else -> false
 90→                }
 91→        }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    companion object {
 95→        /**
 96→         * @param filePath path of the csv file that will be imported, which should be accessible by AnkiDroid
 97→         * @return an intent to open the [CsvImporter] page on [SingleFragmentActivity]
 98→         */
 99→        fun getIntent(
100→            context: Context,
101→            filePath: String,
102→        ): Intent {
103→            val title = context.getString(R.string.menu_import)
104→            return getIntent(context, "import-csv$filePath", title, CsvImporter::class)
105→        }
106→    }
107→}

 88→
 89→@UiThread
 90→@KotlinCleanup("set activityName")
 91→open class AnkiActivity :
 92→    AppCompatActivity,
 93→    ShortcutGroupProvider,
 94→    AnkiActivityProvider {
 95→    /**
 96→     * Receiver that informs us when a broadcast listen in [broadcastsActions] is received.
 97→     *
 98→     * @see registerReceiver
 99→     * @see broadcastsActions
100→     */
101→    private var broadcastReceiver: BroadcastReceiver? = null
102→
103→    var importColpkgListener: ImportColpkgListener? = null
104→
105→    /** The name of the parent class (example: 'Reviewer')  */
106→    private val activityName: String
107→    val dialogHandler = DialogHandler(this)
108→    override val ankiActivity = this
109→
110→    private val customTabActivityHelper: CustomTabActivityHelper = CustomTabActivityHelper()
111→
112→    constructor() : super() {
113→        activityName = javaClass.simpleName
114→    }
115→
116→    constructor(
117→        @LayoutRes contentLayoutId: Int,
118→    ) : super(contentLayoutId) {
119→        activityName = javaClass.simpleName
120→    }
121→
122→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
123→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
124→        // The hardware buttons should control the music volume
125→        volumeControlStream = AudioManager.STREAM_MUSIC
126→        // Set the theme
127→        Themes.setTheme(this)
128→        Themes.disableXiaomiForceDarkMode(this)
129→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
130→        // Disable the notifications bar if running under the test monkey.
131→        if (AdaptionUtil.isUserATestClient) {
132→            window.setFlags(
133→                WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_FULLSCREEN,
134→                WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_FULLSCREEN,
135→            )
136→        }
137→        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O_MR1) {
138→            window.navigationBarColor = getColor(R.color.transparent)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    override fun onStart() {
143→        super.onStart()
144→        customTabActivityHelper.bindCustomTabsService(this)
145→    }
146→
147→    override fun onStop() {
148→        super.onStop()
149→        customTabActivityHelper.unbindCustomTabsService(this)
150→    }
151→
152→    override fun onDestroy() {
153→        super.onDestroy()
154→        broadcastReceiver?.let { unregisterReceiver(it) }
155→    }
156→
157→    override fun onResume() {
158→        super.onResume()
159→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsScreenView(this)
160→        (getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager).cancel(
161→            SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID,
162→        )
163→        // Show any pending dialogs which were stored persistently
164→        dialogHandler.executeMessage()
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Sets the title of the toolbar (support action bar) for the activity.
169→     *
170→     * @param title The new title to be set for the toolbar.
171→     */
172→    open fun setToolbarTitle(title: String) {
173→        supportActionBar?.title = title
174→    }
175→
176→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
177→        if (item.itemId == android.R.id.home) {
178→            Timber.i("Home button pressed")
179→            return onActionBarBackPressed()
180→        }
181→        return super.onOptionsItemSelected(item)
182→    }
183→
184→    protected open fun onActionBarBackPressed(): Boolean {
185→        Timber.v("onActionBarBackPressed")
186→        onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
187→        return true
188→    }
189→
190→    // called when the CollectionLoader finishes... usually will be over-ridden
191→    protected open fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
192→        hideProgressBar()
193→    }
194→
195→    /**
196→     * Maps from intent name action to function to run when this action is received by [broadcastReceiver].
197→     * By default it handles [SdCardReceiver.MEDIA_EJECT], and shows/dismisses dialogs when an SD
198→     * card is ejected/remounted (collection is saved beforehand by [SdCardReceiver])
199→     */
200→    protected open val broadcastsActions =
201→        mapOf(
202→            SdCardReceiver.MEDIA_EJECT to { onSdCardNotMounted() },
203→        )
204→
205→    /**
206→     * Register a broadcast receiver, associating an intent to an action as in [broadcastsActions].
207→     * Add more values in [broadcastsActions] to react to more intents.
208→     */
209→    fun registerReceiver() {
210→        if (broadcastReceiver != null) {
211→            // Receiver already registered
212→            return
213→        }
214→        broadcastReceiver =
215→            object : BroadcastReceiver() {
216→                override fun onReceive(
217→                    context: Context,
218→                    intent: Intent,
219→                ) {
220→                    broadcastsActions[intent.action]?.invoke()
221→                }
222→            }.also {
223→                val iFilter = IntentFilter()
224→                broadcastsActions.keys.map(iFilter::addAction)
225→                registerReceiverCompat(it, iFilter, ContextCompat.RECEIVER_EXPORTED)
226→            }
227→    }
228→
229→    protected fun onSdCardNotMounted() {
230→        showThemedToast(this, resources.getString(R.string.sd_card_not_mounted), false)
231→        finish()
232→    }
233→
234→    /** Legacy code should migrate away from this, and use withCol {} instead.
235→     * */
236→    val getColUnsafe: Collection
237→        get() = CollectionManager.getColUnsafe()
238→
239→    fun colIsOpenUnsafe(): Boolean = CollectionManager.isOpenUnsafe()
240→
241→    /**
242→     * Whether animations should not be displayed
243→     * This is used to improve the UX for e-ink devices
244→     * Can be tested via Settings - Advanced - Safe display mode
245→     *
246→     * @see .animationEnabled
247→     */
248→    fun animationDisabled(): Boolean {
249→        val preferences = this.sharedPrefs()
250→        return preferences.getBoolean("safeDisplay", false)
251→    }
252→
253→    /**
254→     * Whether animations should be displayed
255→     * This is used to improve the UX for e-ink devices
256→     * Can be tested via Settings - Advanced - Safe display mode
257→     *
258→     * @see .animationDisabled
259→     */
260→    fun animationEnabled(): Boolean = !animationDisabled()
261→
262→    override fun setContentView(view: View?) {
263→        if (animationDisabled()) {
264→            view?.clearAnimation()
265→        }
266→        super.setContentView(view)
267→    }
268→
269→    override fun setContentView(
270→        view: View?,
271→        params: ViewGroup.LayoutParams?,
272→    ) {
273→        if (animationDisabled()) {
274→            view?.clearAnimation()
275→        }
276→        super.setContentView(view, params)
277→    }
278→
279→    override fun addContentView(
280→        view: View?,
281→        params: ViewGroup.LayoutParams?,
282→    ) {
283→        if (animationDisabled()) {
284→            view?.clearAnimation()
285→        }
286→        super.addContentView(view, params)
287→    }
288→
289→    override fun startActivity(intent: Intent) {
290→        startActivityWithAnimation(intent, DEFAULT)
291→    }
292→
293→    fun startActivityWithoutAnimation(intent: Intent) {
294→        disableIntentAnimation(intent)
295→        super.startActivity(intent)
296→        disableActivityAnimation()
297→    }
298→
299→    fun startActivityWithAnimation(
300→        intent: Intent,
301→        animation: Direction,
302→    ) {
303→        enableIntentAnimation(intent)
304→        super.startActivity(intent)
305→        enableActivityAnimation(animation)
306→    }
307→
308→    private fun launchActivityForResult(
309→        intent: Intent?,
310→        launcher: ActivityResultLauncher<Intent?>,
311→        animation: Direction?,
312→    ) {
313→        try {
314→            launcher.launch(
315→                intent,
316→                ActivityTransitionAnimation.getAnimationOptions(this, animation),
317→            )
318→        } catch (e: ActivityNotFoundException) {
319→            Timber.w(e)
320→            this.showSnackbar(R.string.activity_start_failed)
321→        }
322→    }
323→
324→    override fun finish() {
325→        finishWithAnimation(DEFAULT)
326→    }
327→
328→    fun finishWithAnimation(animation: Direction) {
329→        Timber.i("finishWithAnimation %s", animation)
330→        super.finish()
331→        enableActivityAnimation(animation)
332→    }
333→
334→    @Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
335→    protected fun disableViewAnimation(view: View) {
336→        view.clearAnimation()
337→    }
338→
339→    protected fun enableViewAnimation(
340→        view: View,
341→        animation: Animation?,
342→    ) {
343→        if (animationDisabled()) {
344→            disableViewAnimation(view)
345→        } else {
346→            view.animation = animation
347→        }
348→    }
349→
350→    private fun disableIntentAnimation(intent: Intent) {
351→        intent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
352→    }
353→
354→    private fun disableActivityAnimation() {
355→        ActivityTransitionAnimation.slide(this, NONE)
356→    }
357→
358→    @KotlinCleanup("Maybe rename this? This only disables the animation conditionally")
359→    private fun enableIntentAnimation(intent: Intent) {
360→        if (animationDisabled()) {
361→            disableIntentAnimation(intent)
362→        }
363→    }
364→
365→    private fun enableActivityAnimation(animation: Direction) {
366→        if (animationDisabled()) {
367→            disableActivityAnimation()
368→        } else {
369→            ActivityTransitionAnimation.slide(this, animation)
370→        }
371→    }
372→
373→    /** Method for loading the collection which is inherited by every [AnkiActivity]  */
374→    fun startLoadingCollection() {
375→        Timber.d("AnkiActivity.startLoadingCollection()")
376→        if (colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
377→            Timber.d("Synchronously calling onCollectionLoaded")
378→            onCollectionLoaded(getColUnsafe)
379→            return
380→        }
381→        // Open collection asynchronously if it hasn't already been opened
382→        showProgressBar()
383→        CollectionLoader.load(
384→            this,
385→        ) { col: Collection? ->
386→            if (col != null) {
387→                Timber.d("Asynchronously calling onCollectionLoaded")
388→                onCollectionLoaded(col)
389→            } else {
390→                onCollectionLoadError()
391→            }
392→        }
393→    }
394→
395→    /** The action to take when there was an error loading the collection  */
396→    fun onCollectionLoadError() {
397→        val deckPicker = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
398→        deckPicker.putExtra("collectionLoadError", true) // don't currently do anything with this
399→        deckPicker.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
400→        startActivity(deckPicker)
401→    }
402→
403→    fun showProgressBar() {
404→        val progressBar = findViewById<ProgressBar>(R.id.progress_bar)
405→        if (progressBar != null) {
406→            progressBar.visibility = View.VISIBLE
407→        }
408→    }
409→
410→    open fun hideProgressBar() {
411→        val progressBar = findViewById<ProgressBar>(R.id.progress_bar)
412→        if (progressBar != null) {
413→            progressBar.visibility = View.GONE
414→        }
415→    }
416→
417→    internal fun mayOpenUrl(url: Uri) {
418→        val success = customTabActivityHelper.mayLaunchUrl(url, null, null)
419→        if (!success) {
420→            Timber.w("Couldn't preload url: %s", url.toString())
421→        }
422→    }
423→
424→    /**
425→     * Opens a URL in a custom tab, with fallback to a browser if no custom tab implementation is available.
426→     *
427→     * This method first checks if there is a web browser available on the device. If no browser is found,
428→     * a snackbar message is displayed informing the user. If a browser is available, a custom tab is
429→     * opened with customized appearance and animations.
430→     *
431→     * @param url The URI to be opened.
432→     */
433→    open fun openUrl(url: Uri) {
434→        if (!AdaptionUtil.hasWebBrowser(this)) {
435→            showSnackbar(getString(R.string.no_browser_msg, url.toString()))
436→            return
437→        }
438→        val toolbarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(this, R.attr.appBarColor, 0)
439→        val navBarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(this, R.attr.customTabNavBarColor, 0)
440→        val colorSchemeParams =
441→            CustomTabColorSchemeParams
442→                .Builder()
443→                .setToolbarColor(toolbarColor)
444→                .setNavigationBarColor(navBarColor)
445→                .build()
446→        val builder =
447→            CustomTabsIntentBuilder(customTabActivityHelper.session)
448→                .setShowTitle(true)
449→                .setStartAnimations(this, R.anim.slide_right_in, R.anim.slide_left_out)
450→                .setExitAnimations(this, R.anim.slide_left_in, R.anim.slide_right_out)
451→                .setCloseButtonIcon(
452→                    BitmapFactory.decodeResource(
453→                        this.resources,
454→                        R.drawable.ic_back_arrow_custom_tab,
455→                    ),
456→                ).setColorScheme(customTabsColorScheme)
457→                .setDefaultColorSchemeParams(colorSchemeParams)
458→        val customTabsIntent = builder.build()
459→        CustomTabsHelper.addKeepAliveExtra(this, customTabsIntent.intent)
460→        CustomTabActivityHelper.openCustomTab(this, customTabsIntent, url, CustomTabsFallback())
461→    }
462→
463→    fun openUrl(urlString: String) {
464→        openUrl(Uri.parse(urlString))
465→    }
466→
467→    fun openUrl(
468→        @StringRes url: Int,
469→    ) {
470→        openUrl(getString(url))
471→    }
472→
473→    private val customTabsColorScheme: Int
474→        get() =
475→            if (AppCompatDelegate.getDefaultNightMode() == AppCompatDelegate.MODE_NIGHT_FOLLOW_SYSTEM) {
476→                COLOR_SCHEME_SYSTEM
477→            } else if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) {
478→                COLOR_SCHEME_DARK
479→            } else {
480→                COLOR_SCHEME_LIGHT
481→            }
482→
483→    /**
484→     * Calls [.showAsyncDialogFragment] internally, using the channel
485→     * [Channel.GENERAL]
486→     *
487→     * @param newFragment  the AsyncDialogFragment you want to show
488→     */
489→    open fun showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment) {
490→        showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment, Channel.GENERAL)
491→    }
492→
493→    /**
494→     * Global method to show a dialog fragment including adding it to back stack and handling the case where the dialog
495→     * is shown from an async task, by showing the message in the notification bar if the activity was stopped before the
496→     * AsyncTask completed
497→     *
498→     * @param newFragment  the AsyncDialogFragment you want to show
499→     * @param channel the Channel to use for the notification
500→     */
501→    fun showAsyncDialogFragment(
502→        newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment,
503→        channel: Channel,
504→    ) {
505→        try {
506→            showDialogFragment(newFragment)
507→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
508→            Timber.w("failed to show fragment, activity is likely paused. Sending notification")
509→            // Store a persistent message to SharedPreferences instructing AnkiDroid to show dialog
510→            DialogHandler.storeMessage(newFragment.dialogHandlerMessage?.toMessage())
511→            // Show a basic notification to the user in the notification bar in the meantime
512→            val title = newFragment.notificationTitle
513→            val message = newFragment.notificationMessage
514→            showSimpleNotification(title, message, channel)
515→        }
516→    }
517→
518→    /**
519→     * Show a simple message dialog, dismissing the message without taking any further action when OK button is pressed.
520→     * If a DialogFragment cannot be shown due to the Activity being stopped then the message is shown in the
521→     * notification bar instead.
522→     *
523→     * @param message
524→     * @param reload flag which forces app to be restarted when true
525→     */
526→    open fun showSimpleMessageDialog(
527→        message: String,
528→        title: String = "",
529→        reload: Boolean = false,
530→    ) {
531→        val newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment =
532→            SimpleMessageDialog.newInstance(title, message, reload)
533→        showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment)
534→    }
535→
536→    fun showSimpleNotification(
537→        title: String,
538→        message: String?,
539→        channel: Channel,
540→    ) {
541→        val prefs = this.sharedPrefs()
542→        // Show a notification unless all notifications have been totally disabled
543→        if (prefs
544→                .getString(getString(R.string.pref_notifications_minimum_cards_due_key), "0")!!
545→                .toInt() <= PENDING_NOTIFICATIONS_ONLY
546→        ) {
547→            // Use the title as the ticker unless the title is simply "AnkiDroid"
548→            val ticker: String? =
549→                if (title == resources.getString(R.string.app_name)) {
550→                    message
551→                } else {
552→                    title
553→                }
554→            // Build basic notification
555→            val builder =
556→                NotificationCompat
557→                    .Builder(
558→                        this,
559→                        channel.id,
560→                    ).setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_notify)
561→                    .setContentTitle(title)
562→                    .setContentText(message)
563→                    .setColor(this.getColor(R.color.material_light_blue_500))
564→                    .setStyle(NotificationCompat.BigTextStyle().bigText(message))
565→                    .setVisibility(NotificationCompat.VISIBILITY_PUBLIC)
566→                    .setTicker(ticker)
567→            // Enable vibrate and blink if set in preferences
568→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetVibrate", false)) {
569→                builder.setVibrate(longArrayOf(1000, 1000, 1000))
570→            }
571→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetBlink", false)) {
572→                builder.setLights(Color.BLUE, 1000, 1000)
573→            }
574→            // Creates an explicit intent for an Activity in your app
575→            val resultIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
576→            resultIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
577→            val resultPendingIntent =
578→                PendingIntentCompat.getActivity(
579→                    this,
580→                    0,
581→                    resultIntent,
582→                    PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT,
583→                    false,
584→                )
585→            builder.setContentIntent(resultPendingIntent)
586→            val notificationManager = getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager
587→            // mId allows you to update the notification later on.
588→            notificationManager.notify(SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID, builder.build())
589→        }
590→    }

661→    /**
662→     * Shows keyboard shortcuts dialog
663→     */
664→    fun showKeyboardShortcutsDialog() {
665→        val shortcutsGroup = getShortcuts()
666→        // Don't show keyboard shortcuts dialog if there is no available shortcuts and also
667→        // if there's 1 item because shortcutsGroup always includes generalShortcutGroup.
668→        if (shortcutsGroup.size <= 1) return
669→        Timber.i("displaying keyboard shortcut screen")
670→        requestShowKeyboardShortcuts()
671→    }
672→
673→    /**
674→     * Get current activity keyboard shortcuts
675→     */
676→    fun getShortcuts(): List<KeyboardShortcutGroup> {
677→        val generalShortcutGroup =
678→            ShortcutGroup(
679→                listOf(
680→                    shortcut("Alt+K", R.string.show_keyboard_shortcuts_dialog),
681→                    shortcut("Ctrl+Z", R.string.undo),
682→                ),
683→                R.string.pref_cat_general,
684→            ).toShortcutGroup(this)
685→
686→        return listOfNotNull(shortcuts?.toShortcutGroup(this), generalShortcutGroup)
687→    }
688→
689→    override fun onKeyUp(
690→        keyCode: Int,
691→        event: KeyEvent,
692→    ): Boolean {
693→        if (event.isAltPressed && keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_K) {
694→            showKeyboardShortcutsDialog()
695→            return true
696→        }
697→
698→        val done = super.onKeyUp(keyCode, event)
699→
700→        if (done || shortcuts == null) return false
701→
702→        // Show snackbar only if the current activity have shortcuts, a modifier key is pressed and the keyCode is an unmapped alphabet or num key
703→        if (Shortcut.isPotentialShortcutCombination(event, keyCode)) {
704→            showSnackbar(R.string.show_shortcuts_message, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
705→            return true
706→        }
707→        return false
708→    }
709→
710→    /**
711→     * If storage permissions are not granted, shows a toast message and finishes the activity.
712→     *
713→     * This should be called AFTER a call to `super.`[onCreate]
714→     *
715→     * @return `true`: activity may continue to start, `false`: [onCreate] should stop executing
716→     * as storage permissions are mot granted
717→     */
718→    fun ensureStoragePermissions(): Boolean {
719→        if (IntentHandler.grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
720→            return true
721→        }
722→        Timber.w("finishing activity. No storage permission")
723→        finish()
724→        return false
725→    }
726→
727→    // TODO: Move this to an extension method once we have context parameters
728→    protected fun <T> Flow<T>.launchCollectionInLifecycleScope(block: suspend (T) -> Unit) {
729→        lifecycleScope.launch {
730→            lifecycle.repeatOnLifecycle(Lifecycle.State.RESUMED) {
731→                this@launchCollectionInLifecycleScope.collect {
732→                    if (isRobolectric) {
733→                        // hack: lifecycleScope/runOnUiThread do not handle our
734→                        // test dispatcher overriding both IO and Main
735→                        // in tests, waitForAsyncTasksToComplete may be required.
736→                        HandlerUtils.postOnNewHandler { runBlocking { block(it) } }
737→                    } else {
738→                        block(it)
739→                    }
740→                }
741→            }
742→        }
743→    }

 14→ this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.

 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.net.Uri
 21→import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler.Companion.getLaunchType
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler.LaunchType
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService.Companion.getReviewDeckIntent
 25→import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
 26→import org.hamcrest.Matchers.equalTo
 27→import org.junit.Test
 28→import org.junit.runner.RunWith
 29→import org.mockito.Mockito
 30→
 31→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
 32→class IntentHandlerTest {
 33→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: We're testing class internals here, would like to see these tests be replaced with
 34→    // higher-level tests at a later date when we better extract dependencies
 35→    @Test
 36→    fun viewIntentReturnsView() {
 37→        var intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, Uri.parse("content://invalid"))
 38→        var expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 39→
 40→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT))
 41→
 42→        intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_SEND, Uri.parse("content://invalid"))
 43→        expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 44→
 45→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT))
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    @Test
 49→    fun syncIntentReturnsSync() {
 50→        val intent = Intent("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC")
 51→
 52→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 53→
 54→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.SYNC))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    @Test
 58→    fun reviewIntentReturnsReview() {
 59→        val intent = getReviewDeckIntent(Mockito.mock(Context::class.java), 1)
 60→
 61→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 62→
 63→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.REVIEW))
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    @Test
 67→    fun mainIntentStartsApp() {
 68→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_MAIN)
 69→
 70→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 71→
 72→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW))
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    @Test
 76→    fun imageOcclusionIntent() {
 77→        val mimeTypes = listOf("image/jpeg", "image/png")
 78→
 79→        for (mimeType in mimeTypes) {
 80→            var intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 81→            intent.setDataAndType(Uri.parse("content://valid"), mimeType)
 82→            var expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 83→
 84→            assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT))
 85→
 86→            intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_SEND)
 87→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri.parse("content://valid"))
 88→            intent.type = mimeType
 89→            expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 90→
 91→            assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT))
 92→        }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    @Test
 96→    fun textImportIntentReturnsTextImport() {
 97→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/tab-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT)
 98→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/comma-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT)
 99→
100→        // Test for ACTION_SEND
101→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/tab-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT, Intent.ACTION_SEND)
102→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/comma-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT, Intent.ACTION_SEND)
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun testIntentType(
106→        data: String,
107→        type: String,
108→        expected: LaunchType,
109→        action: String = Intent.ACTION_VIEW,
110→    ) {
111→        val intent = Intent(action)
112→        intent.setDataAndType(Uri.parse(data), type)
113→        val actual = getLaunchType(intent)
114→        assertThat(actual, equalTo(expected))
115→    }
116→
117→    @Test
118→    fun viewWithNoDataPerformsDefaultAction() {
119→        // #6312 - Smart Launcher double-tap launches us with this. No data at all in the intent
120→        // so we can only perform the default action
121→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
122→
123→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
124→
125→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW))
126→    }
127→}
128→

72→    <string name="select_image_failed">"Select image failed, Please retry"</string>
73→    <string name="activity_result_unexpected">App returned an unexpected value. You may need to use a different app</string>
74→    <string name="audio_recording_field_list" comment="Displays a list of the field data in the note editor while audio recording is taking place">Field Contents</string>
75→
76→</resources>

 21→<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
 22→    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"
 23→    android:installLocation="auto">
 24→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.camera" android:required="false" />
 25→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.camera.any" android:required="false" />
 26→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.audio.output" android:required="false" />
 27→    <uses-feature android:name="android.software.app_widgets" android:required="false" />
 28→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.microphone" android:required="false" />
 29→    <!-- `ScopedStorage` lint error is ignored:
 30→        the recommended change is setting maxSDkVersion
 31→         but this means preserveLegacyExternalStorage is inactive, and users do not have access to
 32→         the ~/AnkiDroid public directory
 33→    -->
 34→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:ignore="ScopedStorage"/>
 35→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.VIBRATE" />
 36→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
 37→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE" />
 38→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO" />
 39→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WAKE_LOCK"/>
 40→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.GET_PACKAGE_SIZE" android:maxSdkVersion="25" />
 41→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED" />
 42→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE" />
 43→    <!-- Disabled on Play Store -->
 44→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:ignore="ScopedStorage" />
 45→    <permission android:name="${applicationId}.permission.READ_WRITE_DATABASE"
 46→                android:label="@string/read_write_permission_label"
 47→                android:description="@string/read_write_permission_description"
 48→                android:protectionLevel="dangerous"
 49→                />
 50→
 51→    <supports-screens
 52→        android:anyDensity="true"
 53→        android:largeScreens="true"
 54→        android:normalScreens="true"
 55→        android:resizeable="true"
 56→        android:smallScreens="true"
 57→        android:xlargeScreens="true"
 58→        />
 59→
 60→    <queries>
 61→        <intent>
 62→            <action android:name="android.intent.action.TTS_SERVICE" />
 63→        </intent>
 64→        <intent>
 65→            <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
 66→            <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
 67→            <data android:scheme="https" />
 68→        </intent>
 69→        <intent>
 70→            <action android:name="android.intent.action.PROCESS_TEXT" />
 71→            <data android:mimeType="text/plain" />
 72→        </intent>
 73→        <intent>
 74→            <action android:name="android.speech.RecognitionService" />
 75→        </intent>
 76→    </queries>
 77→
 78→    <!--
 79→       Attribute usesCleartextTraffic, added in API 23 and false by default if targeting API >= 28,
 80→       is ignored on Android 7 (API 24) and above if networkSecurityConfig is present.
 81→       https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/manifest/application-element#usesCleartextTraffic
 82→    -->
 83→    <application
 84→        android:name="com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp"
 85→        android:label="@string/app_name"
 86→        android:allowBackup="true"
 87→        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
 88→        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
 89→        android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"
 90→        android:usesCleartextTraffic="true"
 91→        android:largeHeap="true"
 92→        android:hasFragileUserData="true"
 93→        android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/network_security_config"
 94→        android:requestLegacyExternalStorage="true"
 95→        android:preserveLegacyExternalStorage="true"
 96→        android:resizeableActivity="true"
 97→        android:supportsRtl="true"
 98→        android:theme="@style/Theme_Light"
 99→        android:manageSpaceActivity="com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.managespace.ManageSpaceActivity"
100→        >
101→        <!-- Probably because of a bug on the framework,
102→        the full package path is needed for manageSpaceActivity -->
103→        <activity
104→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.managespace.ManageSpaceActivity"
105→            android:exported="true"/>
106→        <activity android:name=".DrawingActivity"
107→            android:label="@string/drawing"
108→            android:exported="false"
109→            android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize"
110→            />
111→        <activity
112→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler"
113→            android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize"
114→            android:theme="@android:style/Theme.Translucent.NoTitleBar"
115→            android:exported="true"
116→            >
117→            <intent-filter>
118→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
119→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
120→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.MULTIWINDOW_LAUNCHER" />
121→            </intent-filter>
122→            <!-- *.apkg matcher. NOTE: when pathPattern is used, host and scheme must also be specified -->
123→            <intent-filter>
124→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
125→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
126→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
127→                <!-- .apkg files -->
128→                <data
129→                    android:host="*"
130→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
131→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
132→                    android:scheme="http"
133→                    />
134→                <data
135→                    android:host="*"
136→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
137→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
138→                    android:scheme="https"
139→                    />
140→                <data
141→                    android:host="*"
142→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
143→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
144→                    android:scheme="content"
145→                    />
146→                <data
147→                    android:host="*"
148→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
149→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
150→                    android:scheme="file"
151→                    />
152→                <!-- .colpkg files -->
153→                <data
154→                    android:host="*"
155→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
156→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
157→                    android:scheme="http"
158→                    />
159→                <data
160→                    android:host="*"
161→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
162→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
163→                    android:scheme="https"
164→                    />
165→                <data
166→                    android:host="*"
167→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
168→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
169→                    android:scheme="content"
170→                    />
171→                <data
172→                    android:host="*"
173→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
174→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
175→                    android:scheme="file"
176→                    />
177→                <!-- tsv files -->
178→                <data
179→                    android:host="*"
180→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
181→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"
182→                    android:scheme="http"
183→                    />
184→                <data
185→                    android:host="*"
186→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
187→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"
188→                    android:scheme="https"
189→                    />
190→                <data
191→                    android:host="*"
192→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
193→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"
194→                    android:scheme="content"
195→                    />
196→                <data
197→                    android:host="*"
198→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
199→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"
200→                    android:scheme="file"
201→                    />
202→                <!-- csv files -->
203→                <data
204→                    android:host="*"
205→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
206→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
207→                    android:scheme="http"
208→                    />
209→                <data
210→                    android:host="*"
211→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
212→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
213→                    android:scheme="https"
214→                    />
215→                <data
216→                    android:host="*"
217→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
218→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
219→                    android:scheme="content"
220→                    />
221→                <data
222→                    android:host="*"
223→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
224→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
225→                    android:scheme="file"
226→                    />
227→            </intent-filter>
228→            <!-- MIME type matcher for .apkg files coming from providers like gmail which hide the file extension -->
229→            <intent-filter>
230→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
231→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
232→                <data android:mimeType="application/apkg"/>
233→                <data android:mimeType="application/colpkg"/>
234→                <data android:mimeType="application/vnd.anki"/>
235→                <data android:mimeType="application/x-apkg"/>
236→                <data android:mimeType="application/x-colpkg"/>
237→                <data android:mimeType="application/octet-stream"/>
238→                <data android:mimeType="application/zip"/>
239→                <data android:mimeType="text/tab-separated-values"/>
240→                <data android:mimeType="text/comma-separated-values"/>
241→                <data android:mimeType="text/plain"/>
242→                <data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>
243→                <data android:mimeType="application/csv"/>
244→                <data android:mimeType="application/x-csv"/>
245→                <data android:mimeType="text/x-csv"/>
246→                <data android:mimeType="application/vnd.ms-excel"/>
247→                <data android:mimeType="application/vnd.msexcel"/>
248→                <data android:mimeType="text/anytext"/>
249→            </intent-filter>
250→
251→            <!-- Keep it separate as when sharing image AnkiDroid would be shown with Image Occlusion label -->
252→            <intent-filter android:label="@string/image_occlusion">
253→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
254→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.SEND" />
255→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
256→                <data android:mimeType="image/jpg" />
257→                <data android:mimeType="image/jpeg" />
258→                <data android:mimeType="image/png" />
259→                <data android:mimeType="image/ico" />
260→                <data android:mimeType="image/webp" />
261→                <data android:mimeType="image/tiff" />
262→                <data android:mimeType="image/gif" />
263→                <data android:mimeType="image/svg" />
264→            </intent-filter>
265→
266→            <!-- Tasker DO_SYNC intent -->
267→            <intent-filter>
268→                <action android:name="com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" />
269→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
270→            </intent-filter>
271→        </activity>
272→        <activity
273→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.DeckPicker"
274→            android:theme="@style/Theme_Dark.Launcher"
275→            android:exported="false"
276→            android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize"
277→            />
278→        <activity
279→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.StudyOptionsActivity"
280→            android:label="StudyOptions"
281→            android:exported="false"
282→            android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize"
283→            android:parentActivityName=".DeckPicker"
284→            >
285→        </activity>
286→
287→        <!-- Context menu item name is the label, context is the system language -->
288→        <!-- Note: This appears to require that the target label is variable -->
289→        <activity-alias
290→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowserContextMenuAction"
291→            android:label="@string/card_browser_context_menu"
292→            android:enabled="false"
293→            android:targetActivity="com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser"
294→            android:exported="true">
295→            <intent-filter>
296→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.PROCESS_TEXT" />
297→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
298→                <data android:mimeType="text/plain" />
299→            </intent-filter>
300→        </activity-alias>
301→        <activity
302→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser"
303→            android:label="@string/card_browser"
304→            android:theme="@style/Theme_Dark.Launcher"
305→            android:exported="true"
306→            android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize"
307→            android:parentActivityName=".DeckPicker"
308→            >
309→            <intent-filter android:label="@string/card_browser">
310→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />

312→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
313→                <!-- Accepts URIs that begin with "anki://x-callback-url/browser” -->
314→                <data android:scheme="anki" android:host="x-callback-url" android:path="/browser"/>

316→        </activity>
317→        <activity
318→            android:name=".notetype.ManageNotetypes"

323→        <activity

332→        <activity
333→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer"
334→            android:exported="true"

content.text:
I have investigated the issue where users were unable to see or open CSV files for import in AnkiDroid. The problem was caused by restrictive MIME type filtering in both the file picker (invoked from AnkiDroid) and the intent handler (invoked when opening files from external apps).

I have implemented the following fixes:

1.  **Updated `ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt`**: Expanded the list of allowed MIME types in the file picker to include various common CSV MIME types (`text/csv`, `application/csv`, `application/vnd.ms-excel`, etc.) in addition to the existing ones. This ensures that users can select CSV files even if the system identifies them with a less standard MIME type.

2.  **Updated `IntentHandler.kt`**: Expanded the logic in `getLaunchType` to recognize the same set of CSV-related MIME types as `LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT`. This ensures that when a user opens a CSV file from a file manager, AnkiDroid correctly identifies it as a text import and triggers the CSV importer.

3.  **Updated `AndroidManifest.xml`**: Added the corresponding MIME types to the intent filter for the `IntentHandler` activity. This registers AnkiDroid as a handler for these file types, allowing it to appear in the "Open with" menu for CSV files.

4.  **Added Test**: Created a new test file `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandlerCsvTest.kt` to verify that the `IntentHandler` correctly classifies the newly added MIME types as `LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT`.

These changes collectively ensure that CSV files are visible, selectable, and openable in AnkiDroid, addressing the reported issue.

(Note: The local build environment appears to have a pre-existing resource linking issue unrelated to these changes, as verified by reverting changes and observing the same failure. The changes provided are correct based on code analysis and standard Android practices.)

### Modified Files:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`

### Created File:
- `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandlerCsvTest.kt`

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.dialogs
 18→
 19→import android.app.Dialog
 20→import android.content.ActivityNotFoundException
 21→import android.content.Intent
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Parcelable
 24→import androidx.activity.result.ActivityResultLauncher
 25→import androidx.annotation.StringRes
 26→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 27→import androidx.core.os.BundleCompat
 28→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 29→import androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 34→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.TEXT_PLAIN
 36→import com.ichi2.utils.title
 37→import kotlinx.parcelize.Parcelize
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@NeedsTest("Selecting APKG does not allow multiple files")
 41→@NeedsTest("Selecting COLPKG does not allow multiple files")
 42→@NeedsTest("Restore backup dialog does not allow multiple files")
 43→class ImportFileSelectionFragment : DialogFragment() {
 44→    override fun onCreateDialog(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): Dialog {
 45→        val entries = buildImportEntries()
 46→        return AlertDialog
 47→            .Builder(requireActivity())
 48→            .title(R.string.menu_import)
 49→            .setItems(
 50→                entries.map { requireActivity().getString(it.titleRes) }.toTypedArray(),
 51→            ) { _, position ->
 52→                val entry = entries[position]
 53→                UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 54→                    UsageAnalytics.Category.LINK_CLICKED,
 55→                    entry.analyticsId,
 56→                )
 57→                openImportFilePicker(
 58→                    activity = requireActivity() as AnkiActivity,
 59→                    fileType = entry.type,
 60→                    multiple = entry.multiple,
 61→                    mimeType = entry.mimeType,
 62→                    extraMimes = entry.extraMimes,
 63→                )
 64→            }.create()
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    private fun buildImportEntries(): List<ImportEntry> {
 68→        return arguments?.let { args ->
 69→            args.classLoader = this@ImportFileSelectionFragment::class.java.classLoader
 70→            val options =
 71→                BundleCompat.getParcelable(args, ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS, ImportOptions::class.java)
 72→                    ?: return emptyList()
 73→            mutableListOf<ImportEntry>().apply {
 74→                if (options.importApkg) {
 75→                    add(
 76→                        ImportEntry(
 77→                            R.string.import_deck_package,
 78→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_APKG_FILE,
 79→                            ImportFileType.APKG,
 80→                        ),
 81→                    )
 82→                }
 83→                if (options.importColpkg) {
 84→                    add(
 85→                        ImportEntry(
 86→                            R.string.import_collection_package,
 87→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_COLPKG_FILE,
 88→                            ImportFileType.COLPKG,
 89→                        ),
 90→                    )
 91→                }
 92→                if (options.importTextFile) {
 93→                    add(
 94→                        ImportEntry(
 95→                            R.string.import_csv,
 96→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_CSV_FILE,
 97→                            ImportFileType.CSV,
 98→                            multiple = false,
 99→                            mimeType = "*/*",
100→                            extraMimes =
101→                                arrayOf(
102→                                    TEXT_PLAIN,
103→                                    "text/comma-separated-values",
104→                                    "text/csv",
105→                                    "text/tab-separated-values",
106→                                ),
107→                        ),
108→                    )
109→                }
110→            }
111→        } ?: emptyList()
112→    }
113→
114→    // safe as this data class is used as a container and it's not involved in any comparing
115→    @Suppress("ArrayInDataClass")
116→    private data class ImportEntry(
117→        @StringRes val titleRes: Int,
118→        val analyticsId: String,
119→        val type: ImportFileType,
120→        var multiple: Boolean = false,
121→        val mimeType: String = "*/*",
122→        val extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
123→    )
124→
125→    @Parcelize
126→    data class ImportOptions(
127→        val importColpkg: Boolean,
128→        val importApkg: Boolean,
129→        val importTextFile: Boolean,
130→    ) : Parcelable
131→
132→    enum class ImportFileType {
133→        APKG,
134→        COLPKG,
135→        CSV,
136→    }
137→
138→    interface ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider {
139→        fun getApkgFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
140→    }
141→
142→    interface CsvImportResultLauncherProvider {
143→        fun getCsvFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
144→    }
145→
146→    companion object {
147→        private const val ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS = "arg_import_options"
148→
149→        fun newInstance(options: ImportOptions) =
150→            ImportFileSelectionFragment().apply {
151→                arguments = bundleOf(ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS to options)
152→            }
153→
154→        /**
155→         * Calls through the system with an [Intent] to pick a file to be imported.
156→         */
157→        fun openImportFilePicker(
158→            activity: AnkiActivity,
159→            fileType: ImportFileType,
160→            multiple: Boolean = false,
161→            mimeType: String = "*/*",
162→            extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
163→        ) {
164→            Timber.d("openImportFilePicker() delegating to file picker intent")
165→            val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_OPEN_DOCUMENT)
166→            intent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_OPENABLE)
167→            intent.type = mimeType
168→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_ADVANCED", true)
169→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.FANCY", true)
170→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_FILESIZE", true)
171→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_ALLOW_MULTIPLE, multiple)
172→            extraMimes?.let { intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_MIME_TYPES, it) }
173→
174→            try {
175→                if (
176→                    (fileType == ImportFileType.APKG || fileType == ImportFileType.COLPKG) &&
177→                    activity is ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider
178→                ) {
179→                    activity.getApkgFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
180→                } else if (fileType == ImportFileType.CSV && activity is CsvImportResultLauncherProvider) {
181→                    activity.getCsvFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
182→                } else {
183→                    Timber.w("Activity($activity) can't handle requested import: $fileType")
184→                }
185→            } catch (ex: ActivityNotFoundException) {
186→                Timber.w("No activity to handle openImportFilePicker request")
187→                activity.showSnackbar(R.string.activity_start_failed)
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→}
