190→    // called when the CollectionLoader finishes... usually will be over-ridden
191→    protected open fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
192→        hideProgressBar()
193→    }
194→
195→    /**
196→     * Maps from intent name action to function to run when this action is received by [broadcastReceiver].
197→     * By default it handles [SdCardReceiver.MEDIA_EJECT], and shows/dismisses dialogs when an SD
198→     * card is ejected/remounted (collection is saved beforehand by [SdCardReceiver])
199→     */
200→    protected open val broadcastsActions =
201→        mapOf(
202→            SdCardReceiver.MEDIA_EJECT to { onSdCardNotMounted() },
203→        )
204→
205→    /**
206→     * Register a broadcast receiver, associating an intent to an action as in [broadcastsActions].
207→     * Add more values in [broadcastsActions] to react to more intents.
208→     */
209→    fun registerReceiver() {
210→        if (broadcastReceiver != null) {
211→            // Receiver already registered
212→            return
213→        }
214→        broadcastReceiver =
215→            object : BroadcastReceiver() {
216→                override fun onReceive(
217→                    context: Context,
218→                    intent: Intent,
219→                ) {
220→                    broadcastsActions[intent.action]?.invoke()
221→                }
222→            }.also {
223→                val iFilter = IntentFilter()
224→                broadcastsActions.keys.map(iFilter::addAction)
225→                registerReceiverCompat(it, iFilter, ContextCompat.RECEIVER_EXPORTED)
226→            }
227→    }

245→     *
246→     * @see .animationEnabled
247→     */
248→    fun animationDisabled(): Boolean {
249→        val preferences = this.sharedPrefs()

252→

254→     * Whether animations should be displayed
255→     * This is used to improve the UX for e-ink devices
256→     * Can be tested via Settings - Advanced - Safe display mode
257→     *
258→     * @see .animationDisabled
259→     */

261→
262→    override fun setContentView(view: View?) {
263→        if (animationDisabled()) {
264→            view?.clearAnimation()
265→        }
266→        super.setContentView(view)
267→    }
268→
269→    override fun setContentView(
270→        view: View?,
271→        params: ViewGroup.LayoutParams?,
272→    ) {
273→        if (animationDisabled()) {
274→            view?.clearAnimation()
275→        }
276→        super.setContentView(view, params)
277→    }
278→
279→    override fun addContentView(
280→        view: View?,
281→        params: ViewGroup.LayoutParams?,
282→    ) {
283→        if (animationDisabled()) {

424→    /**
425→     * Opens a URL in a custom tab, with fallback to a browser if no custom tab implementation is available.
426→     *
427→     * This method first checks if there is a web browser available on the device. If no browser is found,
428→     * a snackbar message is displayed informing the user. If a browser is available, a custom tab is
429→     * opened with customized appearance and animations.
430→     *
431→     * @param url The URI to be opened.
432→     */
433→    open fun openUrl(url: Uri) {
434→        if (!AdaptionUtil.hasWebBrowser(this)) {
435→            showSnackbar(getString(R.string.no_browser_msg, url.toString()))
436→            return
437→        }
438→        val toolbarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(this, R.attr.appBarColor, 0)
439→        val navBarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(this, R.attr.customTabNavBarColor, 0)
440→        val colorSchemeParams =
441→            CustomTabColorSchemeParams
442→                .Builder()
443→                .setToolbarColor(toolbarColor)
444→                .setNavigationBarColor(navBarColor)
445→                .build()
446→        val builder =
447→            CustomTabsIntentBuilder(customTabActivityHelper.session)
448→                .setShowTitle(true)
449→                .setStartAnimations(this, R.anim.slide_right_in, R.anim.slide_left_out)
450→                .setExitAnimations(this, R.anim.slide_left_in, R.anim.slide_right_out)
451→                .setCloseButtonIcon(
452→                    BitmapFactory.decodeResource(
453→                        this.resources,
454→                        R.drawable.ic_back_arrow_custom_tab,
455→                    ),
456→                ).setColorScheme(customTabsColorScheme)
457→                .setDefaultColorSchemeParams(colorSchemeParams)
458→        val customTabsIntent = builder.build()
459→        CustomTabsHelper.addKeepAliveExtra(this, customTabsIntent.intent)
460→        CustomTabActivityHelper.openCustomTab(this, customTabsIntent, url, CustomTabsFallback())
461→    }

493→    /**
494→     * Global method to show a dialog fragment including adding it to back stack and handling the case where the dialog
495→     * is shown from an async task, by showing the message in the notification bar if the activity was stopped before the
496→     * AsyncTask completed
497→     *
498→     * @param newFragment  the AsyncDialogFragment you want to show
499→     * @param channel the Channel to use for the notification
500→     */
501→    fun showAsyncDialogFragment(
502→        newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment,
503→        channel: Channel,
504→    ) {
505→        try {
506→            showDialogFragment(newFragment)
507→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
508→            Timber.w("failed to show fragment, activity is likely paused. Sending notification")
509→            // Store a persistent message to SharedPreferences instructing AnkiDroid to show dialog
510→            DialogHandler.storeMessage(newFragment.dialogHandlerMessage?.toMessage())
511→            // Show a basic notification to the user in the notification bar in the meantime
512→            val title = newFragment.notificationTitle
513→            val message = newFragment.notificationMessage
514→            showSimpleNotification(title, message, channel)
515→        }
516→    }

521→     * notification bar instead.
522→     *
523→     * @param message
524→     * @param reload flag which forces app to be restarted when true
525→     */
526→    open fun showSimpleMessageDialog(
527→        message: String,
528→        title: String = "",
529→        reload: Boolean = false,

531→        val newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment =
532→            SimpleMessageDialog.newInstance(title, message, reload)

536→    fun showSimpleNotification(
537→        title: String,
538→        message: String?,
539→        channel: Channel,
540→    ) {
541→        val prefs = this.sharedPrefs()
542→        // Show a notification unless all notifications have been totally disabled
543→        if (prefs
544→                .getString(getString(R.string.pref_notifications_minimum_cards_due_key), "0")!!
545→                .toInt() <= PENDING_NOTIFICATIONS_ONLY
546→        ) {
547→            // Use the title as the ticker unless the title is simply "AnkiDroid"
548→            val ticker: String? =
549→                if (title == resources.getString(R.string.app_name)) {
550→                    message
551→                } else {
552→                    title
553→                }
554→            // Build basic notification
555→            val builder =
556→                NotificationCompat
557→                    .Builder(
558→                        this,
559→                        channel.id,
560→                    ).setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_notify)
561→                    .setContentTitle(title)
562→                    .setContentText(message)
563→                    .setColor(this.getColor(R.color.material_light_blue_500))
564→                    .setStyle(NotificationCompat.BigTextStyle().bigText(message))
565→                    .setVisibility(NotificationCompat.VISIBILITY_PUBLIC)
566→                    .setTicker(ticker)
567→            // Enable vibrate and blink if set in preferences
568→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetVibrate", false)) {
569→                builder.setVibrate(longArrayOf(1000, 1000, 1000))
570→            }
571→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetBlink", false)) {
572→                builder.setLights(Color.BLUE, 1000, 1000)
573→            }
574→            // Creates an explicit intent for an Activity in your app
575→            val resultIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
576→            resultIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
577→            val resultPendingIntent =
578→                PendingIntentCompat.getActivity(
579→                    this,
580→                    0,
581→                    resultIntent,
582→                    PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT,
583→                    false,
584→                )
585→            builder.setContentIntent(resultPendingIntent)
586→            val notificationManager = getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager
587→            // mId allows you to update the notification later on.
588→            notificationManager.notify(SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID, builder.build())
589→        }
590→    }

689→    override fun onKeyUp(
690→        keyCode: Int,
691→        event: KeyEvent,
692→    ): Boolean {
693→        if (event.isAltPressed && keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_K) {
694→            showKeyboardShortcutsDialog()
695→            return true
696→        }
697→
698→        val done = super.onKeyUp(keyCode, event)
699→
700→        if (done || shortcuts == null) return false
701→
702→        // Show snackbar only if the current activity have shortcuts, a modifier key is pressed and the keyCode is an unmapped alphabet or num key
703→        if (Shortcut.isPotentialShortcutCombination(event, keyCode)) {
704→            showSnackbar(R.string.show_shortcuts_message, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
705→            return true
706→        }
707→        return false
708→    }

728→    protected fun <T> Flow<T>.launchCollectionInLifecycleScope(block: suspend (T) -> Unit) {
729→        lifecycleScope.launch {
730→            lifecycle.repeatOnLifecycle(Lifecycle.State.RESUMED) {
731→                this@launchCollectionInLifecycleScope.collect {
732→                    if (isRobolectric) {
733→                        // hack: lifecycleScope/runOnUiThread do not handle our
734→                        // test dispatcher overriding both IO and Main
735→                        // in tests, waitForAsyncTasksToComplete may be required.
736→                        HandlerUtils.postOnNewHandler { runBlocking { block(it) } }
737→                    } else {
738→                        block(it)
739→                    }
740→                }
741→            }
742→        }
743→    }

 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.net.Uri
 21→import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler.Companion.getLaunchType
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler.LaunchType
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService.Companion.getReviewDeckIntent
 25→import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
 26→import org.hamcrest.Matchers.equalTo
 27→import org.junit.Test
 28→import org.junit.runner.RunWith
 29→import org.mockito.Mockito
 30→
 31→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
 32→class IntentHandlerTest {
 33→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: We're testing class internals here, would like to see these tests be replaced with
 34→    // higher-level tests at a later date when we better extract dependencies
 35→    @Test
 36→    fun viewIntentReturnsView() {
 37→        var intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, Uri.parse("content://invalid"))
 38→        var expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 39→
 40→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT))
 41→
 42→        intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_SEND, Uri.parse("content://invalid"))
 43→        expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 44→
 45→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT))
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    @Test
 49→    fun syncIntentReturnsSync() {
 50→        val intent = Intent("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC")
 51→
 52→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 53→
 54→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.SYNC))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    @Test
 58→    fun reviewIntentReturnsReview() {
 59→        val intent = getReviewDeckIntent(Mockito.mock(Context::class.java), 1)
 60→
 61→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 62→
 63→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.REVIEW))
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    @Test
 67→    fun mainIntentStartsApp() {
 68→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_MAIN)
 69→
 70→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 71→
 72→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW))
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    @Test
 76→    fun imageOcclusionIntent() {
 77→        val mimeTypes = listOf("image/jpeg", "image/png")
 78→
 79→        for (mimeType in mimeTypes) {
 80→            var intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 81→            intent.setDataAndType(Uri.parse("content://valid"), mimeType)
 82→            var expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 83→
 84→            assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT))
 85→
 86→            intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_SEND)
 87→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri.parse("content://valid"))
 88→            intent.type = mimeType
 89→            expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 90→
 91→            assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT))
 92→        }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    @Test
 96→    fun textImportIntentReturnsTextImport() {
 97→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/tab-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT)
 98→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/comma-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT)
 99→
100→        // Test for ACTION_SEND
101→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/tab-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT, Intent.ACTION_SEND)
102→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/comma-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT, Intent.ACTION_SEND)
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun testIntentType(
106→        data: String,
107→        type: String,
108→        expected: LaunchType,
109→        action: String = Intent.ACTION_VIEW,
110→    ) {
111→        val intent = Intent(action)
112→        intent.setDataAndType(Uri.parse(data), type)
113→        val actual = getLaunchType(intent)
114→        assertThat(actual, equalTo(expected))
115→    }
116→
117→    @Test
118→    fun viewWithNoDataPerformsDefaultAction() {
119→        // #6312 - Smart Launcher double-tap launches us with this. No data at all in the intent
120→        // so we can only perform the default action
121→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
122→
123→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
124→
125→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW))
126→    }
127→}
